title: Lavender linen water
subtitle: A distilled water and lavender spray for ironing and bedding
excerpt: A lavender linen water for ironing and freshening bedding: distilled water, a splash of vodka to solubilise the essential oil, and lavender essential oil. Spritz onto sheets, pillowcases, and clothing before ironing. The lavender scent on freshly ironed linen releases with body heat as you sleep.
sourceNotes: Traditional European linen water practice. Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014), lavender in fabric-contact applications.

content.text: A lavender linen water. Spritz it onto fabric before ironing. The iron then glides more easily and sets the lavender scent into the cloth. Spritz onto bedding before sleeping; the scent releases gently with body heat in the night. Lavender is the traditional choice for its mild calming effect. The vodka helps the oil mix evenly into the water, so the spray comes out uniform rather than as oily spots.

Distilled water is important here: tap water minerals leave white spots on dark fabrics, especially when the water evaporates quickly under a hot iron. For ironing, use this spray on natural fibres only; linen, cotton, and cotton-linen blends. On synthetics, the liquid can cause steam-pressing marks. Test on an inside seam of a polyester blend before using on the visible surface.

items.name: Vodka
items.prepNote: 40% ABV vodka. 10% of the spray. The solubiliser for the essential oil; also contributes mild preservation.

items.name: Distilled water
items.prepNote: distilled or deionised water. 88% of the spray. Tap water leaves mineral spots on fabric when ironed; distilled water does not.

items.name: Lavender essential oil
items.prepNote: Lavandula angustifolia lavender essential oil. 2% of the spray. Approximately 50 drops. The fragrance active.

content.text: Measure the vodka into the spray bottle. Add the lavender essential oil and swirl to dissolve; the alcohol dissolves the oil before the water is added. Add the distilled water and fit the trigger-spray top. Shake for 15 seconds to distribute evenly. Label with the contents, date, and a note to shake before use.

Shake the bottle. For ironing: spritz lightly onto the fabric (2-3 sprays per item), then iron at the appropriate temperature for the fabric. For bedding: spritz 3-4 times over the pillowcase and top sheet 10-15 minutes before getting into bed. The spray dries to nothing visible but leaves the lavender fragrance in the fabric. Avoid spraying heavily; the fabric should be barely damp, not wet.

Safe for: cotton, linen, cotton-linen blends, and most natural fibres. Test on an inside seam first with any fabric you are unsure about; spritz, allow to dry fully, and check for any marks or colour change before using on the visible surface. Extra caution with: silk (the water can mark the surface; use very sparingly), dark synthetic fabrics (spray lightly and allow to dry before ironing).
3 months at room temperature, away from sunlight. Make in small batches and use regularly; the lavender fragrance is at its freshest in the first 6-8 weeks. Discard if the spray smells flat or sour.
